compound_stress_inference.orderly_vs_disorderly: Currently ORDERLY but deteriorating rapidly. The stress is severe — EMERGENCY on one leg (Brent), extreme ELEVATED on a second (USD/JPY), and UNOBSERVABLE on the third (JGB 10Y). Markets are selling in an orderly fashion ($1T/day equity losses, measured ETF outflows, Fear & Greed at 12 but not at 0). However, the margin between orderly and disorderly is thin: USD/JPY is 0.1 from CRITICAL, Brent may already be above $110, and the JGB leg is invisible. The structure is maximally pre-loaded on observable legs. The velocity of deterioration (Brent from $103.79 to reportedly $110 in ~4 days, S&P losing $1T in a single day) suggests accelerating stress.
compound_stress_inference.catalyst_proximity: Immediate. Any of the following could trigger cascade: (1) USD/JPY crossing 160 — 0.1 away; (2) Brent sustained above $115 — possibly underway; (3) JGB 10Y revealed above 2.5% when data becomes available — unknown; (4) BOJ forced intervention — would spike JGB volatility; (5) Further military escalation at Hormuz — ongoing conflict. The system is one catalyst away from cascade conditions on observable data. The JGB data gap means cascade may have already begun on the invisible third leg.
compound_stress_inference.pre_load_assessment: SEVERELY PRE-LOADED. Two of three legs are at extreme stress levels. Brent at EMERGENCY creates direct yen pressure through Japan's oil import dependency. USD/JPY at extreme ELEVATED (159.9) means the BOJ is approaching the point where intervention becomes unavoidable but intervention itself creates JGB volatility risk. The pre-loading dynamic means: even a small shock on the third leg (JGB) would complete the cascade triangle. And conversely: the Brent and USD/JPY stress are likely ALREADY pressuring JGB yields upward through the fiscal channel (higher oil imports → wider trade deficit → weaker fiscal position → higher sovereign risk premium → higher JGB yields). We just can't see it because the data is 55 days stale.
compound_stress_inference.thesis_paradox: The thesis paradox is at maximum activation. The compound stress chain threatens the position through four channels: (1) risk-off destroys crypto adoption metrics and ETF AUM; (2) macro crisis diverts institutional attention from new infrastructure deployment; (3) Japan corridor stress delays or reverses SBI/BOJ digital settlement progress; (4) RLUSD contraction accelerates as demand for all risk assets declines. SIMULTANEOUSLY, the same stress chain validates the thesis through the channel the thesis was built to address: existing settlement infrastructure (SWIFT, correspondent banking, central bank bilateral clearing) fails under exactly this kind of compound geopolitical stress. The demand for alternative settlement infrastructure is highest precisely when the ability to build and deploy it is lowest. This is not a contradiction to be resolved — it is the structural tension at the heart of the thesis. Layer 4 must state this paradox without resolving it toward bullish or bearish.

compound_stress.pre_loaded_detail: Two of three legs are in extreme stress territory. Brent is at EMERGENCY ($103.79 confirmed, $110 reported). USD/JPY is at extreme ELEVATED (159.9, 0.1 from CRITICAL). JGB 10Y is unassessable due to 55-day data staleness. The structure is severely pre-loaded: with Brent at EMERGENCY and USD/JPY at extreme ELEVATED, any deterioration in any leg could trigger cascade conditions. The break point of a pre-loaded structure is lower than an unloaded structure — the system is effectively operating at CRITICAL on two observable legs even though one leg is technically still ELEVATED (not yet CRITICAL). The third leg (JGB) is the critical unknown: if JGB 10Y has risen above 2.5% during the 55-day gap, the system may already be at EMERGENCY. I cannot determine this without current data. Per the Compound Stress Matrix: 'OR any Hormuz disruption event' automatically qualifies as CRITICAL. CoinDesk's 'Iran War' characterization and confirmed oil price emergency satisfy this condition independently.
compound_stress.stress_chain_proximity: Extremely close to full cascade scenario. Two of three required conditions for EMERGENCY are met or exceeded (Hormuz disruption confirmed, BOJ intervention pressure at threshold). The third condition (JGB 10Y >2.5%) is unobservable. The system is either at high CRITICAL or already at EMERGENCY — the JGB data gap is the only thing preventing definitive classification. The thesis paradox is now maximally active: the violent unwind scenario simultaneously threatens the position through macro cascade AND validates the thesis premise that existing settlement infrastructure fails under stress.
